raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section advances a contribution claim — structural fleet-size invariance for the combined strategy and a two-axis distribution-shift taxonomy — that must be evaluated for internal coherence and adequacy as a framing device.","R1.2":"The causal reasoning chains are dense and load-bearing: the mechanistic explanation for why the combined strategy is invariant (aggregate-distribution / per-vehicle decomposition), the rolling re-planning account for PPO/CMDP, and the training-distribution extrapolation account for predictive LP all require logical scrutiny; a flaw in any invalidates the section's conclusions.","R1.3":"Multiple specific quantitative claims are asserted (100 %, 40–50 %, 91 %→40 % transition at N=1000) without an in-text figure or table reference; verifying that cited numbers are grounded in reported experimental evidence is a central review obligation.","R1.6":"'Review this section' plausibly includes prose quality; the text is dense with technical hedges and some sentences carry heavy parallel structure that may obscure meaning for the intended audience."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize technical/logical soundness of the causal claims and evidence grounding (making R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing), or is it primarily a prose and clarity review (making R1.6 load-bearing), or both equally?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section advances a contribution claim — structural fleet-size invariance for the combined strategy and a two-axis distribution-shift taxonomy — that must be evaluated for internal coherence and adequacy as a framing device.
rationale.R1.2: The causal reasoning chains are dense and load-bearing: the mechanistic explanation for why the combined strategy is invariant (aggregate-distribution / per-vehicle decomposition), the rolling re-planning account for PPO/CMDP, and the training-distribution extrapolation account for predictive LP all require logical scrutiny; a flaw in any invalidates the section's conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific quantitative claims are asserted (100 %, 40–50 %, 91 %→40 % transition at N=1000) without an in-text figure or table reference; verifying that cited numbers are grounded in reported experimental evidence is a central review obligation.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' plausibly includes prose quality; the text is dense with technical hedges and some sentences carry heavy parallel structure that may obscure meaning for the intended audience.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical/logical soundness of the causal claims and evidence grounding (making R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing), or is it primarily a prose and clarity review (making R1.6 load-bearing), or both equally?
